'''Distraction''' was a [[Music/Legends|musical]] [[Band/Legends|group]].

==History==
The [[Band/Legends|band's]] members were the [[Ushka Et]], [[Enni Anni]], [[Va'Lonis]], and [[Tzeel]]. Et was the lead [[Musician/Legends|singer]], Anni played the [[Kloo horn/Legends|Kloo horn]], Va'Lonis played the keyboard and Tzeel was the percussionist. The [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] [[Zim Zimley]] was the band's manager and the [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] [[Niklas Cories]] also worked for them, under the alias of "Aphus Polix". The band toured the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] and on one occasion had a concert scheduled aboard the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]], ''[[Sleemo Poya Jeedai]]''.

The retired musician [[Quoltus Firth]] pulled some strings and hired some [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] to worked as security for the band during the event, as he wanted them to search for Cories, who had previously dated his daughter. The spacers met with the band's regular security guards, the [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] brothers [[Xydge]], [[Rydge]], and [[Stydge]], before being introduced to the band. As part of a security ploy, Xydge then escorted [[Veris]], [[Gindo]], [[Essara]], and [[Rossi Kildaon]], the band's doubles, off the ship. Cories planned to attack the Hutt ship during the concert, so he killed Tzeel's double and then secretly Ushka Et's one, before capturing and tying up the the real Et. He hoped that the spacers would deduce that Tzeel had been killed and replaced by her double, when actually Cories was posing as Et. To make this seem true, he made sure that Tzeel took some [[Spice/Legends|spice]] before the show, so that she wouldn't perform well and so make it seem more likely that she was her double. The concert began with Cories posing as Et. He planned to throw a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] into the crowd and so kill many of them and cripple the ship.

The band performed very slow and depressing music, leading to more than one live [[Death/Legends|suicide]] during their performances.
